headline: Is Citigroup Stock Outperforming the Dow?

headline: Dropbox Completes New Senior Secured Revolving Credit Facility; Announces $900M Stock Repurchase Program

headline: JPMorgan sees further upside for Mag-7, but not a repeat of 2025
summary: Investing.com -- JPMorgan maintained its constructive stance on the Magnificent Seven in a note to clients on Monday, arguing that the group's derating earlier this year has left room for further gains even as the bank stops short of calling a repeat of last year's narrow, tech-driven rally.

headline: Arbor Realty Trust (ABR) Valuation Check After CLO Redemption And Liquidity-Boosting Refinancing
summary: Arbor Realty Trust (ABR) recently redeemed $787.0 million of legacy CLO 17 notes and moved about $1.21 billion of assets into repurchase facilities with JPMorgan, securing lower pricing and higher leverage. See our latest analysis for Arbor Realty Trust. The refinancing news comes after a volatile stretch for the stock, with the share price up 1.95% over the last day and 3.23% over the past week, but down 27.49% over 30 days and with the 1 year total shareholder return falling 31.11%. This...

headline: JPMorgan's Bullish Q2 Outlook Highlights Fee Income Potential
summary: JPM's Q2 2026 fee income will likely surge, driven by a likely rise of 11% in markets revenue and 10% jump in IB fees, fueled by volatility and deal-making.

headline: Investors Need to Realize Silver Supply Bailouts Are No Longer Available
summary: In September 2020, JP Morgan paid a landmark $920 million settlement to resolve charges of “spoofing” – placing and cancelling fake buy or sell orders – to manipulate the silver market. A little over five years later, JP Morgan closed out a 3.17 million oz. silver short position (633 delivery notices), conveniently right before silver ... Investors Need to Realize Silver Supply Bailouts Are No Longer Available

headline: Trump's Scandals Are Muddying CLARITY's Chances, Mike Novogratz Says—But Do Prediction Markets Agree?
summary: Mike Novogratz cut his CLARITY Act passage odds from 90% to 60%, blaming Trump's $1.776 billion slush fund and $250 bill push.

headline: Binance Ventures Into US Stocks Trading For Overseas Customers, Eyes Tokenized Shares In 'Super App' Push
summary: Binance announced its entry into the U.S. stocks and ETFs trading market on Monday, with overseas users set to gain access to more than 7,000 U.S.-listed stocks and exchange-traded funds.

headline: Regulators Didn’t Speak to US Fund at Heart of Segantii Trial
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The insider trading trial of hedge fund Segantii Capital Management enters a critical moment this week, as the prosecution wraps up its side of the case following testimony from an official at Hong Kong’s securities regulator.
